question_text: In the India map shown below, the highlighted states are linked by which major national transport corridor?

answer_key.explanation: The corridor connecting Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata via four-lane / six-lane highways is the Golden Quadrilateral.

visual.caption: Four-metro corridor states highlighted.
visual.altText: Political outline of India with four geographically distant states shaded — one in the north, one in the west, one in the south, and one in the east.
